channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump freezes aid to South Africa, promotes resettlement of refugees facing race discrimination
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order freezing all aid to South Africa and pledging to promote resettlement of refugees facing race discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California approves $50M to protect immigrants and defend state against Trump administration
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has signed laws to help safeguard the state's progressive policies from challenges by Republican President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Newsom signs $50M 'Trump-proofing' legislative package
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom called a special legislative session shortly after President Donald Trump's electoral victory to bolster the state's legal defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota joins lawsuit against Trump’s move to restrict gender-affirming care
item.description: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison, along with attorneys general from Washington and Oregon and three unnamed doctors, filed a federal lawsuit Friday calling for a halt on a presidential executive order that would end federal funding to medical institutions that provide gender-affirming care.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders freeze of aid to South Africa, citing country's land expropriation law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Friday formalizing his announcement earlier this week that he’ll freeze assistance to South Africa for a law aiming to address some of the wrongs of South Africa’s racist apartheid era — a law the White House says amounts to discrimi
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells Ishiba at the White House that he wants to slash the US trade deficit with Japan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday he wants to slash the U.S. trade deficit with Japan as he welcomed Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba to the White House for their first face-to-face meeting.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarm over borrowers' personal information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are pushing back against Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising concerns about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republican introduces transgender lawmaker as 'the gentleman ... Mr. McBride' for floor speech
item.description: Rep. Mary Miller called Rep. Sarah McBride, who identifies as a transgender woman, "the gentleman from Delaware, Mr. McBride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats ask for an investigation into DOGE's access to Treasury's payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are seeking a Treasury Department investigation of the access that Elon Musk's team was given to the government's payment system, citing “threats to the economy and national security, and the potential violation of laws protecting Americans’ privacy and tax dat
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official's directive tying transportation grants to birth rates could hinder blue states
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Shortly after he was confirmed as President Donald Trump’s transportation secretary, Sean Duffy circulated a memo that instructed his department to prioritize families by, among other things, giving preference to communities with marriage and birth rates higher than the national avera
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY and SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate moves full steam ahead on massive Trump budget bill after GOP divisions derail House
item.description: Senate Republicans are moving ahead with a bill to pass President Donald Trump's agenda on a range of issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red states pursue their own DOGE-style reforms
item.description: Numerous Republican-led states are seeking to implement measures to slash the size and cost of state governments in an effort to mirror the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: GOP state attorneys general campaign group brings back leader who resigned in aftermath of Jan. 6
item.description: An organization with the mission of electing Republicans to state attorney general positions is bringing back a former executive director who resigned in the aftermath of the attack on the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth on priorities, vision for 2025 session
item.description: Under a new power-sharing deal, Demuth will be speaker at least through next year. She spoke with MPR News host Cathy Wurzer.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, Ngoc Bui, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Senate installs Russell Vought as Trump’s next budget chief
item.description: The Senate voted 53-47 along party lines to confirm Project 2025 architect Russell Vought on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'Iron Dome for America' a major undertaking: Newton
item.description: A proposed $2.5 trillion "Iron Dome for America" is expected to be much more complex than its Israel-based counterpart
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota distillery alarmed by now-delayed Canadian tariffs, retaliatory measures
item.description: The Princeton-based company exports about 5 percent of its products, including vodkas, brandies and liqueurs, to Canada. There, the company’s Butter Ripple Schnapps is used in a drink named after American actor Burt Reynolds.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court hears arguments on House conflict despite GOP, DFL agreement
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court heard oral arguments Thursday morning in a case involving a standoff in the Minnesota House that has dragged on for more than three weeks, though GOP and DFL leaders had reached an agreement Wednesday night to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: As 'Fork' resignation deadline arrives, a federal judge will consider a stay
item.description: The Trump administration has given more than 2 million federal employees until Thursday to decide whether to stay or go. A last-minute court hearing may throw a wrench into the administration's plans.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: New attorney general moves to align Justice Department with Trump's priorities
item.description: Trump and Attorney General Pam Bondi have both argued the Justice Department under Biden unfairly targeted conservatives, most notably Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Simon says in House goes, but temporary chamber leader tiptoes through intensely partisan terrain
item.description: Secretary of State Steve Simon is in the center of a fight over power in the Minnesota House of Representatives. The executive branch officeholder who was first elected to public office two decades ago says he’s not taking sides. Republicans have sued Simon and vice versa.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Freshman Democrat, physician Rep. Morrison warns against RFK Jr. nomination
item.description: Minnesota Third-District Democratic Rep. Kelly Morrison, who is one of a handful of physicians in Congress, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to express her concerns and what Kennedy’s confirmation could mean in practice.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. appears on track to become US health secretary as he wins key Republican senator's support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal vaccine skeptic and activist lawyer, appeared on track to become the nation's health secretary after winning the crucial support of Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC
